commit.message:
fix: prevent UnicodeDecodeError on /errors when stored data contains invalid UTF-8 bytes (#205)

* Initial plan

* fix: sanitize non-UTF-8 bytes in ERROR_SOURCES_SQL using toValidUTF8 and mapApply

Applies toValidUTF8() to all String scalar columns (ServiceName, TraceId,
SpanId, Body) and mapApply((k,v)->(k,toValidUTF8(v))) to LogAttributes map
values in ERROR_SOURCES_SQL. This prevents chDB's sqlitelike execute from
crashing with UnicodeDecodeError when stored error data contains raw non-UTF-8
byte sequences (e.g. truncated multi-byte characters from OTLP agents).

Adds regression test: injects a row with invalid UTF-8 bytes via unhex() and
verifies both normal and grouped /errors pages return HTTP 200.

fix(jetson): fix Docker volume permissions in setup script

Docker container runs as UID created inside the container (agrikd),
which differs from host's agrikd UID. Mounted volumes were only
chown'd to host user, making them inaccessible inside the container.

Fix: After chown, apply chmod to ensure:
- Read-only mounts (config, models): 755 dirs, 644 files
- Read-write mounts (data, logs): 777 dirs
- config.json: 644 after every write (credential injection)
- engine files: 644 after download/conversion

commit.message:
fix: -j/--json flag, TOTP hidden by default, search via get-logins, clip/totp accept URL (#4)

* fix: UX improvements — -j/--json flag, TOTP visibility, search via get-logins, clip/totp by URL

- Replace global --format {table,json,tsv} with per-subcommand -j/--json flag.
  The flag can be placed anywhere after the subcommand name, which is the
  standard behaviour for CLI tools.

- output.py: TOTP is now hidden in 'show' output unless -p/--show-password is
  passed. TSV format removed (unused).

- search: Changed from get_database_entries + client-side filter to
  get_logins(query), which works without the 'Allow access to all entries'
  permission in KeePassXC Browser Integration settings. The query is matched
  against URLs/hostnames by KeePassXC.

- totp: Now accepts a URL (like 'show') instead of a UUID. The entry is
  resolved via get_logins and the UUID is passed to get_totp internally.

- clip: 'field' is now a positional argument instead of --field.
  New usage: keepassxc-cli clip <password|username|totp> <url>

- pyproject.toml: bump keepassxc-browser-api requirement to >=0.1.3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- README: rewrite all command examples to match current interface (-j/--json,
  clip <field> <url>, totp <url>, corrected generate options, edit --url required,
  ls permission note, remove tsv references)

- edit: replace get_database_entries with get_logins(url); --url is now required.
  This removes the dependency on the 'Allow access to all entries' KeePassXC
  setting. Current field values are fetched via get_logins so omitted args are
  left unchanged as before.

- ls: add description noting the 'Allow access to all entries' permission required.

- clip: show entry name (not internal UUID) in 'No TOTP configured' error message.

commit.message:
tui: fix help overlay ANSI code corruption and add proper line styling

The help text (HELP_TEXT_BASE) contains ANSI escape sequences (\x1b[1m,
\x1b[0m) designed for raw terminal output. When passed to ratatui's
rendering pipeline, these bytes leaked through to the terminal, causing
random style corruption (some lines white, some gray, inconsistent).

Fix:
- Add strip_ansi() to remove CSI sequences before ratatui touches the text
- Add style_info_line() for per-line ratatui styling:
  * Section headers (no indent): Cyan Bold
  * Command lines (/cmd [args]  desc): command=White Bold, desc=Muted
  * Tip lines (• ...): Muted
  * Everything else: terminal default

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: fix marketplace timeout — optimise queries + raise axios timeout to 60s

Root causes of 'timeout of 30000ms exceeded' on Marketplace page:
1. listListings() used correlated subqueries (COUNT/MAX per row) — N+1 anti-pattern
2. adminListAllListings() same correlated subquery issue
3. Missing composite indexes on status+admin_approved and bids listing+status
4. Axios global timeout was 30s — free-tier Render DB can take 30-90s to wake

Fixes:
- MarketplaceService: correlated subqueries → aggregated LEFT JOIN (single scan)
- database.ts: seedDefaultSettings creates 4 perf indexes on startup (idempotent)
- api.ts + client.ts: timeout 30000 → 60000ms
- api.ts: added second interceptor to enrich timeout errors with isTimeout flag
  so pages can show 'server waking up' message instead of generic error

commit.message:
feat(multiplayer): rejoin-or-leave UX for active room conflicts

When createRoom() or joinRoom() returns a CONFLICT because the user
already has an active room, include the blocking room's ID in the error
payload (details.activeRoomId) so the client can offer a recovery path.

LobbyEntryDialog now detects this conflict and replaces the plain error
text with an inline prompt offering two options:
- Rejoin: navigates directly to /play/lobby/{activeRoomId}
- Leave & Continue: calls leaveRoom(), then retries the original action

Error handling: if leaveRoom fails, the error message is shown and the
dialog stays open.

AppError gains a dedicated CONFLICT union member with optional
details?: ConflictDetails. The appError() factory is updated with
typed overloads to preserve type safety at call sites.

Closes Story 17.3 — final story of EPIC 17.

tui: fix input text rendered in muted gray instead of terminal default

The user's typed text (text_style in render_input) was being rendered
with MUTED color (Rgb(140,140,140)), making it appear faint and hard
to read. Change to Style::default() so it uses the terminal's native
text color. Only the placeholder and scroll indicators stay muted.

commit.message:
Rename to squad-federation-core, add ARCHITECTURE.md

Rename:
- squad-federated → squad-federation-core (plugin dir, plugin.json,
  marketplace, all cross-references, MCP server name)

Documentation:
- ARCHITECTURE.md: 948-line comprehensive architecture document covering
  all 10 subsystems (git mechanics, signal protocol, knowledge lifecycle,
  launch mechanics, OTel observability, archetype system, ceremony protocol,
  configuration, scripts reference). Includes TypeScript interfaces, ASCII
  diagrams, and protocol specs. Based on original ADRs, generalized.
- README.md: rewritten for three-layer architecture, 2 core agents
  (onboard + sweeper), prompt resolution chain, archetype auto-install

Authorship:
- git config set to lyvladi@gmail.com for this repo
- All commit history rewritten to single author identity

commit.message:
ci: add NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_ENABLED build arg to Dockerfile and CI/CD pipeline

Wire NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_ENABLED as a Docker build arg (default 'false') in:
- Dockerfile: ARG + ENV declarations alongside OPTIMIZER_ENABLED
- ci-cd.yml: all 3 places OPTIMIZER_ENABLED is passed (push build, tag build, ACA deploy)

Reads from NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_ENABLED repo secret; falls back to 'false' if unset.

commit.message:
fix: use was_in_use guard instead of player.active_source

player.active_source is the raw device-reported source and doesn't
reflect plugin sources. Use the pre-captured was_in_use flag (from
PluginSource.in_use_by) which is the authoritative ownership signal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
